race.name: Vastgotaloppet
race.tagline: Two days, two techniques — Sweden's unique back-to-back skate and classic marathon in the heart of Västergötland.

vitals.registration: Open registration; seeding race for Vasaloppet and Tjejvasan
vitals.aid_stations: Aid stations along the course (8km lap format)

climate.description: Ulricehamn in western Sweden has more variable snow conditions than inland Dalarna. Late January/early February temperatures typically range from -10°C to 2°C. Snow reliability can be challenging in milder winters.
challenges: Snow reliability variable in western Sweden's milder climate
Two-day format means adapting to potentially different conditions
Maritime influence can bring above-zero temperatures

course.primary: An 8km lap-based course at Lassalyckan in Ulricehamn, with skate and classic events on consecutive days. One of Sweden's largest Vasaloppet seeding races.
course.surface: Machine-groomed tracks for both skate and classic technique
features: Unique two-day format: skate Saturday, classic Sunday
One of Sweden's largest Vasaloppet seeding races

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Unique two-technique format and strong role as a Vasaloppet seeding race. Ski Classics Challengers status adds prestige. Snow reliability in western Sweden is the main concern.

history.summary: Västgötaloppet emerged in the early 2000s at Lassalyckan in Ulricehamn, western Sweden. It has carved a niche as one of the few events offering both skate and classic marathons on consecutive days. The race serves as one of Sweden's largest seeding races for Vasaloppet and Tjejvasan, and joined the Ski Classics Challengers circuit.
notable_facts: Unique format with skate and classic races on consecutive days
One of Sweden's largest Vasaloppet seeding races
Based at Lassalyckan, Ulricehamn's established Nordic center
Multiple distance options cater to different ability levels
